questions.question_text: What is your full name?
What is your phone number and email address?
questions.hint: Your email should look professional.

questions.question_text: Which city and state are you based in right now?
LinkedIn profile URL (optional)
questions.hint: Leave blank if you don't have one yet.

questions.question_text: GitHub profile URL (optional)
questions.hint: Leave blank if you don't have one yet.

questions.question_text: What degree are you pursuing or have you completed?
What is your branch or specialisation?
questions.hint: e.g. Computer Science and Engineering, Electronics and Communication

questions.question_text: Name of your college or university
Expected graduation year
What is your current CGPA or percentage?
Where did you do your Class 12 (HSC)? What was your percentage or grade?
questions.reassurance: Many companies, especially for fresher roles, still ask for your Class 12 score. Just write the school name and your percentage or grade.

questions.question_text: Which programming languages have you written code in?
questions.hint: Even just for assignments counts! If you wrote a C program in your first semester, select C.
questions.reassurance: Select everything you have ever written code in — no matter how basic or how long ago. Recruiters search resumes for specific language names, so the more complete this is, the better.

questions.question_text: Which tools or platforms have you used?
questions.hint: Even if you only opened it once for a lab session — if you have used it, tick it.

questions.question_text: Which libraries or frameworks have you used?
questions.hint: Even briefly in a tutorial or project counts.

questions.question_text: Do you have any non-technical skills that required real effort to develop?
questions.hint: Think about public speaking, writing, design, teaching, event management, a musical instrument, a sport you trained for.
questions.reassurance: These are more valuable than they seem. A student who can speak confidently or write clearly stands out. Don't skip this just because it feels non-technical.

questions.question_text: Have you ever built anything — an app, website, game, ML model, chatbot, robot, or script — for a course, competition, hackathon, club, or just to learn something?
options: No — just small exercises or nothing yet
questions.hint: Even just for assignments counts!
questions.reassurance: Don't worry about how impressive it sounds or the format of your answer. Just tell us what you built — we'll take care of the rest.

questions.question_text: What was the project called?
What problem did it solve? Who would use it?
questions.hint: Even 'it was a college assignment on linked lists' is fine — just tell us what it did.

questions.question_text: What technologies, languages, or tools did you use?
questions.reassurance: No specific format needed. 'Python and Flask', 'just C++', 'HTML/CSS/JS' — anything works. We'll format it properly.

questions.question_text: Did you build it alone or with a team?
What was your specific role in the project?
questions.hint: e.g. 'Built the ML model and REST API'; or 'I did everything'

questions.question_text: GitHub link or live URL (optional)
Did it win any prize or get any recognition?
Did you do any course projects, mini-projects, or semester projects you are proud of — even if they were just for college submissions?
questions.reassurance: Even a 'basic' college submission belongs here. A library system, a simple calculator app — these show you applied what you learned in class. Don't underestimate them.

questions.question_text: Have you done any internship — paid or unpaid, at a company, startup, research lab, or remotely — even for just 2–4 weeks?
options: No, but I did a virtual or online program
questions.hint: Even a short 2-week internship at a local company counts.

questions.question_text: Company name and what they do
questions.hint: e.g. DeepBlue — AI services startup

questions.question_text: Your role or title
questions.hint: e.g. Software Development Intern

questions.question_text: Duration and year
questions.hint: e.g. June – July 2025 (1 month)

questions.question_text: What did you actually work on day-to-day?
questions.reassurance: Don't just write your job title. Think: what did you build, fix, or improve? Even one specific thing — 'built the login page' or 'helped debug the API' — is more powerful than a vague description.

questions.question_text: Name of the program and platform
questions.hint: e.g. Infosys Springboard — Python for Data Science (4-week virtual internship, 2024)

questions.question_text: Have you done any paid or unpaid work outside of internships?
questions.hint: Think about freelance projects, part-time jobs, tutoring, family business work, content creation.
questions.reassurance: This section is for anything that wasn't a formal internship. Even tutoring a neighbour's kid for a few months, or building a website for a local shop for cash — that is real work experience and belongs on your resume.

questions.question_text: What type of work was it?
Company, client, or organisation name (if any)
questions.hint: e.g. Self-employed / Freelance; or 'Local bakery'

questions.question_text: Your role or title
questions.hint: e.g. Freelance Web Developer; Part-time Cashier; Maths Tutor

questions.question_text: Duration
questions.hint: e.g. June 2024 – August 2024; or 'Ongoing since Jan 2024'

questions.question_text: What did you do? What did you build, deliver, or achieve?
questions.reassurance: Be specific — even one concrete detail makes a huge difference. 'Built a 3-page website for a local restaurant' is stronger than 'did web development'. Format doesn't matter at all — just tell us what you did.

questions.question_text: Have you completed any online courses with a certificate?
questions.hint: Think about Coursera, NPTEL, edX, Udemy, LinkedIn Learning, AWS, Google, or similar platforms.

questions.question_text: Name the course(s), platform, and any grade received
questions.hint: e.g. NPTEL Python for Data Science — IIT Madras, Elite certificate (8 weeks, 2024)

questions.question_text: Have you cleared any industry certification exams?
questions.hint: e.g. AWS Cloud Practitioner, HackerRank, TCS NQT, GATE, Google Analytics

questions.question_text: Which exam and what score or badge?
questions.hint: e.g. HackerRank SQL — Gold badge; AWS Cloud Practitioner 2024

questions.question_text: Have you won, been runner-up, or reached the finals of any competition?
questions.hint: Think about coding contests, hackathons, quiz competitions, debates, sports tournaments, tech fests.

questions.question_text: Name of the competition and what you built or solved
questions.hint: e.g. Smart India Hackathon 2024 internal finalist — built an air quality monitoring system with a team of 4

questions.question_text: Have you been selected for any special program, scholarship, or merit recognition?
questions.hint: e.g. MITACS, DAAD, research fellowships, merit scholarships

questions.question_text: Describe it briefly
questions.hint: e.g. Merit scholarship for top 5% CGPA in 2nd year, APJ KTU

questions.question_text: Have you ranked highly in any exam, contest, or class?
questions.hint: JEE, GATE, state-level tests, LeetCode, Codeforces, CodeChef, or even top of your class.

questions.question_text: Which exam / platform and what rank or percentile?
questions.hint: e.g. JEE Main 97.4 percentile; LeetCode top 10% rating, 250+ problems

questions.question_text: Have you had anything published — a paper, a blog, a GitHub repo that got stars, or an open source contribution?
Describe it and include a link if possible
questions.hint: e.g. Co-authored paper submitted to IEEE ICCSEA 2025 on image segmentation

questions.question_text: Have you worked with any professor, research lab, or research group on a study, experiment, or paper — even as a student helper or volunteer?
questions.hint: Even if you just helped collect data, run experiments, or review papers for a professor — that counts.
questions.reassurance: Research experience is one of the most impressive things on a fresher resume. Even 'I helped my professor collect survey data for 2 weeks' is worth mentioning. Don't skip this if anything comes to mind.

questions.question_text: Professor / lab name and institution
questions.hint: e.g. Under Dr. Priya Nair, NIT Trichy — Computer Vision Lab

questions.question_text: What was the topic or problem you were working on?
questions.hint: e.g. GAN-based image synthesis for medical imaging; or 'I helped with data collection for a survey on student mental health'

questions.question_text: Duration
questions.hint: e.g. Jan – May 2025 (5 months)

questions.question_text: Did it lead to a paper, poster, or presentation?
Tools or methods used
questions.hint: e.g. Python, PyTorch, LaTeX; or 'Google Forms, Excel, SPSS'

questions.question_text: Have you held any official position in a club, student body, or committee at your college?
questions.hint: e.g. President, Vice President, Secretary, Technical Head, Event Coordinator, Class Representative

questions.question_text: Role, organisation, and duration
questions.hint: e.g. Technical Head, IEEE Student Branch, 2023–24

questions.question_text: What did you do in that role?
questions.reassurance: Think about specific things: Did you organise events? Manage a team? Raise funds? Get sponsorships? Teach juniors? Even small contributions like 'managed WhatsApp group and attendance for 60 students' tells a story.

questions.question_text: Have you led a team informally — for a hackathon, project, college event, or any group activity?
questions.hint: You don't need a title. If you were the one who delegated tasks, kept people together, or made decisions for the group — that is leadership.

questions.question_text: Briefly describe it
questions.hint: e.g. Led a 4-person team at a 24-hour internal hackathon; managed task delegation and final presentation

questions.question_text: Are you a member of any technical club, cultural group, sports team, or community at your college or outside it?
Have you attended or organised any tech events, workshops, hackathons, or webinars?
Name the event(s) and your role
questions.hint: e.g. Co-organised TechXplore 2024 — 2-day inter-college tech fest with 400+ participants

questions.question_text: Do you have any creative output — blog, YouTube channel, design portfolio, GitHub projects with followers, or open source contributions?
Describe it and include a link if possible
questions.hint: e.g. Tech blog on Medium — 500+ monthly readers; Figma design portfolio link

questions.question_text: Have you volunteered for any cause, event, or organisation — at college, in your community, or online?
questions.hint: Even one-time events count. Helped at a blood donation camp? Taught juniors? Participated in a clean-up drive?

questions.question_text: What did you do, for how long, and was there a certificate?
questions.hint: e.g. NSS teaching volunteer — helped underprivileged students with maths for 40+ hours; received certificate

questions.question_text: Which languages can you speak and / or write fluently?
questions.hint: Include all — regional languages absolutely count. Malayalam, Tamil, Hindi, Kannada — all of these are valuable.

questions.question_text: What are you genuinely interested in or excited about in tech or your field?
questions.hint: Be honest — not just what sounds impressive. What do you actually enjoy reading about, building, or learning?

questions.question_text: Outside of tech and academics, what do you enjoy doing consistently?
questions.hint: A hobby you have done for over a year is more credible than a one-off.
questions.reassurance: Painting, cooking, chess, music, sport, writing, crafts — all of these are real hobbies worth mentioning. You do not need to justify them as 'useful'.
